events.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
events.description: About 800 Mozambicans said to be caught up in violence in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration protests sweep country Five Mozambique nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend, the Mozambican government said – the first deaths officially linked to protests against illegal immigration sweeping the country. About 800 Mozambican nationals were caught up in violence that broke out in the southern coastal city of Mossel Bay on Friday, a government statement said. Continue reading...

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
events.description: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Follow live here from New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations

events.title: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns

events.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
events.description: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event will have to be suspended,” Filiberto Frausto, a union leader, told AFP, which witnessed police firing teargas on 1 June. “A cause like ours should be far above – it’s far more important than a little bit of distraction and fun.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup

events.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
events.description: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of African farms that have fragmented landscapes and hampered the spread of large savannah fires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

events.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
events.description: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
events.description: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations demanding an immediate ceasefire while Russia insists its strikes target only military infrastructure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting

events.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
events.description: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. Such typhoons are typically characterised by broad areas of low pressure and extensive wind fields, often without a distinct eye. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters

events.title: ‘Not the deal promised’: Labor’s Ed Husic questions Aukus pact that will deliver secondhand subs

The defence minister, Richard Marles, this week agreed to US requests for Australia to accept three second-hand Virginia-class nuclear submarines, rather than a combination of new and old vessels . Continue reading...

events.title: Ben Roberts-Smith won’t see full brief of war crime allegations for months – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The foreign minister, Penny Wong , has announced new sanctions by the commonwealth over three Israeli individuals and four entities in response to “escalating settler violence against Palestinians in the West Bank”. The entities will receive targeted financial sanctions while the individuals will receive travel bans to Australia. For the first time, designated entities now include farming outposts that serve as hubs for settler violence. Settler violence is used to displace Palestinians and perpetuate the settlement enterprise, through destruction of property, displacement of families, beatings, sexual assault, and torture, resulting in serious injuries and deaths. This is another beat up. It’s not unusual in tax legislation for the definitions to be settled in what’s called legislative instruments. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ben Roberts-Smith won’t see full brief of war crime allegations for months – as it happened

The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

events.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
events.description: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist. Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and divided up their plunder. events.title: US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend
events.description: CENTCOM announced that it targeted Iran in "self-defense strikes" during the weekend, asserting that CENTCOM will defend American interests.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military attacks Iran in 'self-defense strikes' over weekend

events.title: Israel eliminates head of Hamas' military wing in Gaza strike
events.description: Israeli forces killed Mohammed Odeh, the new head of Hamas' military wing, in an airstrike in northern Gaza, according to the Israel Defense Forces.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel eliminates head of Hamas' military wing in Gaza strike

events.title: Rafael Grossi: the next Iran nuclear deal will look very different
events.description: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi says the 2015 Iran nuclear deal is no longer a workable model.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Funds for Trump's Board of Peace aren't in World Bank account. So where are they?
events.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Financial Times reporter Abigail Hauslohner about the funding of President Trump's Board of Peace to oversee Gaza's reconstruction.

events.title: 'Vitiforestry': French winemakers plant trees in bid to counter climate change
events.description: Winemakers across France are experimenting with the ancient practice of "vitiforestry" – growing trees alongside vines – to tackle the modern issue of climate change, and many are impressed with the results. As the world faces increasingly unpredictable weather, trees can help protect vines from spring frosts and provide shade during heatwaves.

events.title: 'I gave birth in the street': Conflict makes childbirth risky in parts of Africa
events.description: Women are 40 times more likely to die in pregnancy or childbirth in Central African Republic than in the United States, according to the United Nations

events.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo near 300 as survivors describe their recoveries
events.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

events.title: Iran rejects reports President Masoud Pezeshkian resigned over power struggle
events.description: Over the last 24 hours, reports have surfaced claiming Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian resigned due to an alleged power struggle between his government and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps which is “excluding” him from decision-making in the Iran war. Tehran has outright denied the reports, calling it "rumour mongering" by a foreign media outlet and accusing it of publishing "wishful thinking in place of reality," as the information war continues.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran rejects reports President Masoud Pezeshkian resigned over power struggle

events.title: Trump says Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to dial back fighting
events.description: US President Donald Trump said Monday he had persuaded Israel and Hezbollah to de-escalate, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu agreeing not to send troops to southern Beirut and the Lebanese militant group promising to stop attacks. Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky says that, if true, the move represents a "major coup" for both Hezbollah and Iran.

events.title: 'Not Western version of democracy': 147 constituencies allegedly excluded from Ethiopia's election
events.description: As Ethiopians head to the polls, François Picard is pleased to welcome Jervin Naidoo, Political Analyst at Oxford Economics Africa. While official narratives emphasise stability and democratic progress, significant portions of the country remain excluded from voting, raising concerns about representation, legitimacy, and the consolidation of power. Naidoo argues that while voting has proceeded in much of the country, the exclusion of Tigray and dozens of constituencies elsewhere constitutes "a big problem" that "hurts the government's legitimacy."

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox Israelis have demonstrated across the country against mandatory military enlistment

events.title: Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design
events.description: Blocking children from social media is no substitute for making platforms safe in the first place, the UN human rights office warned Friday, as it issued a 10-point framework urging governments and tech companies to go further and faster to protect children online.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Banning children from social media is not the answer, UN warns – platforms must be made safe by design

events.title: Kash Patel touts FBI 'full-throttle mission' after Operation Spring Cleaning yields 615 indictments/complaints
events.description: A nationwide FBI operation called Spring Cleaning netted over 1,100 arrests and seized hundreds of firearms and massive quantities of drugs.

events.title: South Africa’s World Cup delegation departs for Mexico without coach
events.description: Bafana Bafana's departure was delayed due to non-issuance of visas for several players and support staff.

events.title: India’s Zee Entertainment signs World Cup 2026 broadcast deal with FIFA
events.description: Zee will broadcast the 2026 and 2030 World Cups and the 2027 Women's World Cup among 39 FIFA tournaments until 2034.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Ultra-Orthodox protesters block roads and trains across Israel over military draft
events.description: Tens of thousands of ultra-Orthodox demonstrated across Israel on Monday, blocking roads and trains and setting cars on fire to protest mandatory enlistment in Israel's military.

events.title: U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny
events.description: American aircraft fired on a number of Iranian sites over the weekend, including Qeeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. And, Trump's anti-weaponization fund faces scrutiny in Congress and the courts.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strikes Iranian military sites. And, Trump's $1.8 billion fund faces scrutiny

events.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
events.description: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

events.title: Sabalenka beats Osaka, Berrettini reaches quarter-finals at French Open
events.description: Aryna Sabalenka strengthened her French Open title bid on Monday by defeating Naomi Osaka to reach the quarter-finals, while Matteo Berrettini continued his comeback from injury with a place in his first Grand Slam quarter-final since 2022. The world number one is now the only major champion left in the singles draws at Roland Garros.

events.title: Tactical timeout ban and new VAR powers - major World Cup rule changes announced
events.description: Major rule changes have been announced ahead of the 2026 World Cup - including expanded powers for VAR, 10-second substitutions and a crackdown on tactical timeouts.

events.title: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
events.description: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

events.title: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
events.description: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

events.title: College students accused of inciting boardwalk chaos walk free as prosecutors pull plug, city demands answers
events.description: Charges against four college students accused of organizing a Rehoboth Beach takeover event have been dropped by Delaware's Department of Justice.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 College students accused of inciting boardwalk chaos walk free as prosecutors pull plug, city demands answers

events.title: Six people killed in Iowa domestic dispute, police say
events.description: The suspected shooter was found dead near a bridge with a self-inflicted gunshot wound, authorities say.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Six people killed in Iowa domestic dispute, police say

events.title: ‘Like mice in a cage’: Inside Europe’s prison overcrowding crisis
events.description: In some of Europe’s richest countries, including Belgium, overcrowding is driving a severe deterioration of conditions.

events.title: Hailstorm hits Denver and causes widespread disruption
events.description: A powerful hailstorm swept through the US city of Denver, bringing hailstones reportedly as large as golf balls.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Hailstorm hits Denver and causes widespread disruption

events.title: President Trump attempts to end hostilities in Lebanon
events.description: US President Trump claims a diplomatic breakthrough after speaking with Israeli PM to halt escalation toward Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 President Trump attempts to end hostilities in Lebanon

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's 'forgotten accounts'?
events.description: Billions of euros sit untouched in Germany's dormant bank accounts. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who owns the billions in Germany's 'forgotten accounts'?

events.title: Seeking deal with Iran, Trump promises calm in Lebanon
events.description: US President Donald Trump has said Israel and Hezbollah have agreed that "all shooting will stop." Iran has suspended negotiations as it insists there can be no peace that does not include Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Seeking deal with Iran, Trump promises calm in Lebanon

events.title: Middle East war live: Israeli military intercepts projectiles from Lebanon
events.description: Tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border continued early on Tuesday as Israeli air defences intercepted incoming projectiles, despite US President Donald Trump’s claim that he had brokered an end to fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: Israeli military intercepts projectiles from Lebanon

events.title: 'More than 45' killed after blast at building storing explosives in Myanmar
events.description: Scores of people have been killed following a blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar.

events.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 11 and traps others in damaged buildings
events.description: Russian forces have launched a massive aerial attack on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, killing at least 14 civilians and wounding over 100

events.title: Rescuers dig for bodies after a mining explosives blast in Myanmar kills at least 43
events.description: More than a dozen rescue and charity groups are using excavation machinery to recover bodies following a massive blast from stored mining explosives in northeastern Myanmar

events.title: Florida beach toll booth worker killed after driver rams structure before getting stuck in sand, sheriff says
events.description: A toll booth attendant was killed in Daytona Beach Shores after a pickup truck rammed the structure. The driver allegedly smelled of alcohol.

events.title: Teen athlete arrested, three horses pulled from competition after alleged overnight barn attack
events.description: A teenage competitor allegedly stabbed three horses at a barrel racing event near the South Point Hotel Casino in Las Vegas, facing 12 charges.

events.title: Karmelo Anthony’s self-defense claim could define murder trial in high school track meet stabbing: analyst
events.description: Karmelo Anthony faces trial on a first-degree murder charge in the fatal stabbing of teen Austin Metcalf at a Frisco high school track meet in April 2025.

events.title: Ex-Iowa school superintendent sentenced to prison as an illegal alien found with firearms
events.description: Iowa's largest school district ex-superintendent sentenced to prison for illegally possessing firearms while living in the U.S. as an illegal alien.

events.title: Hezbollah's 'game changing' night-hunting weapon punches through Israel's defenses: expert
events.description: Drone expert warns Hezbollah's nighttime drone capabilities are game changing, using thermal sensors to target IDF troops under cover of darkness.

events.title: English cops cuffed teen stabbing victim after attacker claimed racial assault
events.description: Police apologize after handcuffing stabbing victim Henry Nowak based on attacker's alleged racism claim, sparking outrage over British policing culture.

events.title: Fireworks reportedly trigger mass horse stampede through Rome streets, injuring several soldiers
events.description: Unauthorized fireworks reportedly triggered a mass horse stampede during a late-night rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade in Rome, injuring riders.

events.title: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
events.description: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
events.description: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contacted police after discovering that his bank accounts had been frozen. Continue reading...

events.title: Federal government paid $3.8m in personal protection for then CFMEU administrator
events.description: Senate committee also hears Murray Watt needed personal security after sending the union into administration Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government has paid millions of dollars in personal protection for the administrators of the CFMEU, and had to organise security for minister Murray Watt after he introduced the legislation that placed the union into administration, it has been revealed. Under questioning from Liberal senator Jane Hume in the Senate committee on education and employment legislation, it was revealed that the Department of Employment and Workplace Relations (DEWR) has paid millions of dollars to keep the administrators safe. Continue reading...

events.title: After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild
events.description: After a large-scale Russian missile strike hit downtown Kyiv, the owners of a coffee shop that opened just hours earlier were already serving coffee again and planning to rebuild.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 After Russian missile strike, owners of Ukrainian coffee shop pledge to rebuild

events.title: Most new German citizens keep original nationality: survey
events.description: Germany's previous government changed laws to allow dual citizenship. A new survey shows that, one year after that reform, more than 85% of new German citizens are opting to keep their original nationality.

events.title: EU agrees deal for deporting migrants to third-country 'return hubs'
events.description: The European Union on Monday agreed a deal to allow countries to send migrants ordered to leave the bloc to third-country "return hubs". The legislation, which still requires formal approval by EU governments and the European Parliament, has drawn criticism from human rights groups.

events.title: Pro-Trump candidate lead in Colombia 'part of Donroe doctrine' asserting itself in region
events.description: Pro-Trump lawyer Aberaldo de la Espriella pulled ahead as a leader in Colombia’s race for the presidency in the first round of elections over the weekend, capitalizing on a growing appetite for heavy-handed crackdowns on criminal groups across Latin America. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Christopher Sabatini, Senior Research Fellow on the Americas at Chatham House, says that "this is really again a part of what's unfortunately called the 'Donroe' doctrine asserting itself in partisan politics in Latin America".

events.title: Israel seizes Crusades-era fortress in Lebanon as it expands offensive
events.description: Israeli forces have captured a strategically important fortress from the Crusades, marking the country's deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter of a century.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel seizes Crusades-era fortress in Lebanon as it expands offensive

events.title: Alex Murdaugh murder saga lands right back where it started before next Lowcountry courtroom battle

events.description: IAVI, Moderna and the University of Oxford are all working on new vaccines.

events.title: India news: Mamata Banerjee presses ahead with protest amid turmoil in TMC
events.description: Mamata Banerjee said she would proceed with a sit-in protest in Kolkata, amid escalating tensions within the TMC. DW has more.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Baden-Württemberg restricts smoking in public spaces
events.description: The southwestern German state of Baden-Württemberg has expanded restrictions on smoking in public spaces like outdoor swimming pools, theme parks and zoos.

events.title: Can Iran rebuild trust with its Gulf neighbors?
events.description: Mutual suspicion between Iran and Persian Gulf countries is poised to affect regional stability, growth and cooperation for years.

events.title: If Cuba collapses, Trump will be forced to deal with the consequences of his actions
events.description: It's only a couple of months or so since I was last in Cuba, and conditions then were pretty dire: power cuts, large queues for bread and fuel, state-run grocery stores virtually empty, mounting piles of rubbish on street corners, and few foreign visitors.

events.description: Media freedom advocates condemn move as latest effort to curtail independent reporting on the US military.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: AI startup Anthropic files for IPO after reaching $965 billion valuation
events.description: Anthropic, the firm behind AI assistant Claude, submitted a confidential filing to go public with the US Securities and Exchange Commission on Monday. The firm, valued at close to a trillion dollars, could make its market debut by the end of the year as it sees a surge in interest for its range of AI products. Also in this edition: as France welcomes new data centre investments, we see what's driving that interest and what impact it could have on local communities.

events.title: Nancy Guthrie likely targeted by local worker who assumed family was wealthy, says veteran investigator
events.description: A forensic scientist believes Nancy Guthrie was targeted by a local worker who assumed the family had money due to her daughter Savannah Guthrie.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
events.description: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’ The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election. That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...

events.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
events.description: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks A far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting. With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...

events.description: Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza

events.title: Denmark's Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister
events.description: After her party's weakest showing since 1903, Mette Frederiksen spent months negotiating a coalition to stay in power.

events.title: EU reaches deal on 'return hubs' for rejected asylum-seekers
events.description: The deal involves sending people whose asylum applications have been rejected to third countries. It still requires formal approval to come into effect.

events.title: Serena Williams confirms return to professional tennis
events.description: The tennis icon will play doubles tennis at a grass court tournament this month, marking an expected return to the sport.

events.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
events.description: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

events.title: Is 'nation-hopping' ruining German youth football?
events.description: Many young German footballers are choosing other countries over Germany. DFB Director Andreas Rettig is aiming to change this and is calling for a compensation fee.

events.title: Coups and crises shake African democracy
events.description: Junta leader Ibrahim Traore's call for Burkina Faso to "forget" democracy signaled a political shift across the continent.

events.title: Pancreatic cancer: Dr. Pudlarz praises 'new generation targeted therapy' that inhibits gene mutation
events.description: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Dr. Thomas Pudlarz, Medical Oncologist and Head of the pancreatic care pathway within the 'Interception' program at the Gustave-Roussy Institute. For decades, pancreatic cancer has remained one of oncology's most formidable challenges. Despite major advances in immunotherapy and precision medicine, treatment for pancreatic cancer remains elusive. The emergence of an experimental targeted therapy is "one of the first improvements in many years" for a disease that remains among the most aggressive and deadliest forms of cancer, explains Dr. Pudlarz.

events.title: French Open: Aryna Sabalenka prevails in night session game against Naomi Osaka
events.description: Aryna Sabalenka made easy work of Naomi Osaka (7-5, 6-3) in round of 16. She will face Russia's Diana Shnaider in the quarter-final.

events.title: US says Israel and Hezbollah agree to dial back fighting as tensions escalate
events.description: Tensions between Israel and Hezbollah intensified on Monday, after US President Donald Trump said the two sides had agreed to dial back fighting following talks with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and indirect communication with Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US says Israel and Hezbollah agree to dial back fighting as tensions escalate

events.title: Trump reconsiders $1.8 billion ally compensation fund amid Republican backlash
events.description: US President Donald Trump was said to be reconsidering a controversial $1.8 billion compensation fund on Monday, created as part of a settlement with the IRS, amid legal challenges and growing opposition from Republicans. The Justice Department has paused implementation following a court order, raising doubts about the fund's future.

events.title: Danish PM Frederiksen secures third term with new coalition government
events.description: Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen announced on Monday that she has formed a new left-leaning minority coalition after months of negotiations following inconclusive elections in March. The agreement secures her third term in office, bringing together four parties that hold a slim parliamentary majority.

events.title: Senegal's ousted PM Ousmane Sonko says his party will not participate in new government
events.description: No description available.

events.title: US, UK and Australia to develop underwater drones through defence pact
events.description: Unmanned undersea vehicles will be developed under the AUKUS defence pact, US secretary of defence Pete Hegseth said.

events.title: Social Democrat Frederiksen set to start third term as Denmark's prime minister
events.description: Mette Frederiksen is set to begin a third term as Denmark’s prime minister, leading a center-left coaliton of four parties

events.title: Canada is failing the Jewish community and Jews are being targeted, Carney says
events.description: Canada is failing the Jewish community, Carney says

events.title: Pro-Trump candidate takes lead in Colombia's presidential race
events.description: Pro-Trump lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella has taken the lead in Colombia’s presidential race after the first round of elections over the weekend

events.title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
events.description: Nvidia has unveiled powerful new chips to bring advanced artificial intelligence to Windows laptops and desktops

events.title: Venezuela’s ruling party unity cracks as Delcy Rodríguez shifts Chávez-era policies
events.description: Venezuela's ruling party is facing internal divisions after 27 years of unity

events.title: Some young Spaniards embrace Catholicism and can’t wait for Pope Leo’s visit
events.description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews

events.title: Patients find help with therapy donkeys at psychiatric hospital near Paris
events.description: Therapy donkeys are helping patients with mental health conditions at a psychiatric hospital near Paris

events.title: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
events.description: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps

events.title: Texas teens accused of using dating apps to lure young men into violent robberies that left one victim shot
events.description: Two Texas teens allegedly used online dating platforms to lure victims into robberies, including one that left a 15-year-old boy shot four times, authorities said.

events.title: Oregon cop shot, several dead as gunman barricades himself inside home: 'Unimaginable loss of lives'
events.description: An Oregon police officer was shot multiple times responding to a domestic disturbance incident in Sandy that left multiple victims dead, authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Oregon cop shot, several dead as gunman barricades himself inside home: 'Unimaginable loss of lives'

events.title: Hidden tunnel discovered in Tijuana may have supported cross-border trafficking operations
events.description: Authorities uncover a 265-meter underground tunnel near the U.S.-Mexico border equipped with lighting, ventilation and an electronic transport system.

events.title: Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst
events.description: Iran's supreme leader launched a sweeping counteroffensive against President Donald Trump, seeking to rally Middle Eastern nations into an anti-American alliance, an analyst says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mojtaba Khamenei touts new anti-US alliance as Gulf backchannels seep into Tehran: analyst

events.title: Anti-cartel hardliner channels Trump in bid to end Colombia's leftist era in pivotal election
events.description: Colombia's next president could reshape U.S. counternarcotics cooperation as voters choose between security-first and dialogue-based approaches.
